text:
Once upon a time, in a quaint little village nestled between rolling hills, there lived a wise old owl named Oliver. Every evening, he’d emerge from his cozy tree hole, casting a silvery shadow across the village. Oliver had a remarkable ability: he could hear every bird singing a different song.

One spring day, a new bird moved into town. Its song was different from all the others—a cheerful tune filled with hopefulness. This bird became known as Silverwing.

Silverwing didn’t just sing. Every time it flew over the meadows, it’d flap gently, casting gentle dreams. People stopped and listened. Among them was a young girl named Amelia.

Amelia felt drawn to Silverwing. She’d follow her through oak woods and into meadows. One afternoon, they settled onto a quiet hill that overlooked the village. Silverwing started singing a story about a once lonely caterpillar that became a butterfly. Amelia listened, enchanted.

Through Silverwing’s tales, Amelia felt her
